mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation and risk appetite, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is actively easing. The signal fails if rate cuts occur alongside worsening inflation or labor stress that dominates the discount-rate channel.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which precedes lower corporate sales and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear after a clear labor-market deterioration, not during flat unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's conditional mean return is not below unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening labor income and retail demand, supporting revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when payroll gains are offset by collapsing sales growth.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone for predicting positive 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate stays below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates expanding real output without renewed inflation pressure, which should support equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if industrial production improves only because of price spikes or temporary restocking.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL inflation remains flat or lower over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO/CPIAUCSL condition fails to deliver positive 60d excess returns in at least 55% of triggered episodes, or if returns are negative on average across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in GDPC1 implies slower aggregate demand and weaker earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when output growth decelerates from an already elevated level and fails if GDP revisions later reverse the slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with declining GDPC1 are not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the sign prediction is wrong in more than half of post-decline episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy rates become more negative, supporting risk assets through easier real financial conditions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if higher inflation is interpreted as a growth shock that simultaneously lifts discount rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rises and FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS combination does not produce positive 5d returns more often than not, or if the average 5d return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with falling PAYEMS identifies a genuine labor-market contraction that should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises only because labor supply expands while payrolls remain firm.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS do not lead to negative 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening new-home demand, which spills into cyclical consumption and credit-sensitive sectors before broad equity weakness appears. Over the next 20 trading days, lower housing starts should predict negative S&P 500 returns when the drop is large enough to indicate a real housing slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by below-zero 20d SPX returns on average, or if the signal does not beat a zero-mean benchmark over any 2-year rolling window.
